alphabetItems.explanation: This is a combination of ո and ւ — exactly parallel to the Greek ου. It is how Armenian writes its 'oo' vowel, and many treat it as the alphabet's honorary extra letter.

eastern.full: This ligature reads 'yev' at the start of a word and 'ev' elsewhere. On its own it spells the word 'and.' So, 'արև' is pronounced 'ah-REV.'

western.full: This ligature reads 'yev' at the start of a word and 'ev' elsewhere. On its own it spells the word 'and.' So, 'արև' is pronounced 'ah-REV.'

alphabetItems.explanation: A ligature of ե and ւ. When capitalized it is written as two letters, ԵՎ.
